sections.paragraph:
m. (once f. Zep 2:6), with suff. חַבְלִי pl. חֲבָלִים const. חֶבְלֵי Psa 18:5 116:3 , and חַבְלֵי Jos 17:5

(1) a cord, a rope (Arab. حَبْلُ, Syr. ܚܰܒܠܳ Ethiop. ሐብል፡ To this answer Gr. κάμιλος, French [and English] cable, nor was there ground for the rejection of the Greek word as fictitious, as was done by some philologists, see Passow. Lex. i, 779). Jos 2:15. Ecc 12:6, חֶבֶל הַכֶּסֶף “a cord made of silver threads.”

(2) especially a measuring line, Amo 7:17 2Sa 8:2 hence-(a) a measured field, given to any one by lot, Jos 17:14 19:9 , and thus inheritance, portion, possession. Psal. 16:6 , חֲבָלִים נָֽפְלוּ־לִי בַּנְּעִימִים “a portion has been allotted to me in a pleasant region.” Deu 32:9, יַעֲקֹב חֶבֶל נַחֲלָתֹו “Jacob (is) his possession.” Hence, in a general sense, a tract of land, a region, Deu 3:4 13:14 . חֶבֶל הַיָּם a maritime district, sea-coast, Zep 2:5, Zep 2:6.

(3) a gin, a noose, a snare, Psa 140:6 Job 18:10. חֶבְלֵי מָוֶת שְׁאֹול, “snares of death, of Hades” [if this word and the preceding are to be taken together, (and from the connection with the N. Test. it appears that these occurrences must at least belong to the former,) these expressions signify the pangs (or sorrows) of death, of Hades], Psa 18:5, Psa 18:6 116:3 .

(4) a band of men, a company, 1Sa 10:5, 10 1Sa 10:10(comp. Germ. Bande, Rotte [Engl. band]).

[“(5) destruction (compare Piel No. 2), Mic 2:10, well rendered by the Syr. and Vulg.”]
